title: gp3ml
text: gp3ml governance-first R package leakage-resistant group-aware predictive modelling validation using Gazepoint-derived research data. package designed explicitly observed, non-sensitive outcomes declared scientific purposes. generic automatic machine- learning wrapper perform autonomous model selection.

title: gp3ml
text: package provides: task, purpose, outcome, predictor, identifier-role governance; feature-provenance manifests leakage auditing; deterministic group-aware holdout splitting; repeated grouped resampling materialized folds; fold-balance, coverage, exclusion, outcome diagnostics; fold-local preprocessing contracts; governed statistical optional machine-learning engines; discrimination, error, calibration, uncertainty assessment; external-validation reports; model cards reproducibility reports. Supported predictive-generalization targets : new trials among known participants; new participants; new stimuli; simultaneous new-participant new-stimulus generalization.

title: gp3ml
text: Participant overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization new participants. Stimulus overlap treated failure declared target requires generalization unseen stimuli. Preprocessing must estimated inside relevant analysis partition resampling fold. Package examples tests use deterministic synthetic data. ## Leakage-audit workflow audit_gazepoint_ml_leakage() audits already-defined analysis assessment partitions predictive evaluation. checks: compatibility declared generalization target; participant, participant-trial, stimulus overlap; outcome declared identifiers included predictors; declared target-derived post-outcome predictors; exact row overlap repeated predictor profiles; duplicated rows missing grouping identifiers; identifier-like predictor names requiring manual review. returned audit overall pass, review, fail status, complete check table, non-passing issue table, partition summaries. Audit tables can exported using write_gazepoint_ml_leakage_audit_csv(). audit identifies structural risks visible supplied partitions declared variable roles. establish preprocessing feature selection estimated within resampling folds; safeguards require separate provenance resampling infrastructure.

code: example_data <- expand.grid(   participant_id = sprintf("P%02d", 1:6),   stimulus_id = sprintf("S%02d", 1:4),   repetition = 1:2,   KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) example_data$trial_id <- paste0(   example_data$stimulus_id,   "_T",   example_data$repetition ) participant_number <- as.integer(   sub("P", "", example_data$participant_id) ) stimulus_number <- as.integer(   sub("S", "", example_data$stimulus_id) ) example_data$outcome <- factor(   ifelse(     (participant_number + stimulus_number) %% 2L == 0L,     "review",     "pass"   ),   levels = c("pass", "review") ) row_index <- seq_len(nrow(example_data)) example_data$fixation_duration <- 180 + row_index example_data$pupil_change <- round(   sin(row_index / 7),   4 ) example_data$repetition <- NULL manifest <- create_gazepoint_feature_manifest(   features = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   scientific_source = c(     "Gazepoint fixation export",     "Gazepoint pupil export"   ),   source_table = c("fixations", "pupil"),   transformation = c(     "Trial-level mean",     "Trial-level change"   ),   availability_stage = "during_exposure",   prediction_time_available = TRUE,   preprocessing_scope = "none",   fold_local_required = FALSE ) folds <- create_gazepoint_group_folds(   data = example_data,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   feature_manifest = manifest,   generalization_target = "new_participants",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   v = 3L,   repeats = 1L,   seed = 101L ) audit <- audit_gazepoint_group_folds(folds) audit #> <gazepoint_group_folds_audit> #> Overall status: PASS #> Audited folds: 3 #> Non-passing checks: 0

code: training_data <- data.frame(   participant_id = rep(sprintf("P%02d", 1:12), each = 2),   trial_id = sprintf("T%02d", 1:24),   stimulus_id = rep(c("S01", "S02"), 12),   fixation_duration = 180 + seq_len(24),   pupil_change = sin(seq_len(24) / 3),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) training_data$quality_status <- factor(   c(     "pass", "review", "pass", "review", "review", "pass",     "review", "pass", "pass", "review", "review", "pass",     "review", "pass", "review", "pass", "pass", "review",     "pass", "review", "review", "pass", "pass", "review"   ),   levels = c("pass", "review") ) task <- declare_gazepoint_task(   data = training_data,   outcome = "quality_status",   purpose = "Predict predefined recording-quality review status",   task_type = "classification",   unit_id = "trial_id",   participant_id = "participant_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   generalization_target = "new_participants",   positive = "review" ) model <- train_gazepoint_classifier(   data = training_data,   task = task,   predictors = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   engine = "glm",   seed = 101L ) external_data <- training_data external_data$participant_id <- rep(   sprintf("E%02d", 1:12),   each = 2 ) external_data$trial_id <- sprintf("ET%02d", 1:24) external_data$fixation_duration <-   external_data$fixation_duration + 4 external_data$pupil_change <- code: custom_fit <- function(x, y, task, args) {   training_data <- data.frame(     .outcome = y,     x,     check.names = FALSE   )   stats::glm(     .outcome ~ .,     data = training_data,     family = stats::binomial()   ) } custom_predict <- function(fit, newdata, type, task, ...) {   as.numeric(stats::predict(     fit,     newdata = as.data.frame(newdata),     type = "response"   )) } engine <- integrate_black_box_model(   name = "custom_glm",   fit_fun = custom_fit,   predict_fun = custom_predict,   supports = "classification",   probability = TRUE,   safety_declaration = list(     prohibited_uses_acknowledged = TRUE,     prediction_time_inputs_only = TRUE,     group_aware_evaluation_required = TRUE   ) ) engine #> $name #> [1] "custom_glm" #>  #> $fit_fun #> function (x, y, task, args)  #> { #>     training_data <- data.frame(.outcome = y, x, check.names = FALSE) #>     stats::glm(.outcome ~ ., data = training_data, family = stats::binomial()) #> } #> <environment: 0x0000017760e1f540> #>  #> $predict_fun #> function (fit, newdata, type, task, ...)  #> { #>     as.numeric(stats::predict(fit, newdata = as.data.frame(newdata),  #>         type = "response")) #> } #> <environment: 0x0000017760e1f540> #>  #> $supports #> [1] "classification" #>  #> $probability #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $metadata #> list() #>  #> $safety_declaration #> $safety_declaration$prohibited_uses_acknowledged #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $safety_declaration$prediction_time_inputs_only #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $safety_declaration$group_aware_evaluation_required #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "gp3ml_engine"

title: Print Gazepoint fold diagnostics — print.gazepoint_fold_diagnostics
code: example_data <- expand.grid(   participant_id = sprintf("P%02d", 1:6),   stimulus_id = sprintf("S%02d", 1:4),   repetition = 1:2,   KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) example_data$trial_id <- paste0(   example_data$stimulus_id,   "_T",   example_data$repetition ) participant_number <- as.integer(   sub("P", "", example_data$participant_id) ) stimulus_number <- as.integer(   sub("S", "", example_data$stimulus_id) ) example_data$outcome <- factor(   ifelse(     (participant_number + stimulus_number) %% 2L == 0L,     "review",     "pass"   ),   levels = c("pass", "review") ) row_index <- seq_len(nrow(example_data)) example_data$fixation_duration <- 180 + row_index example_data$pupil_change <- round(   sin(row_index / 7),   4 ) example_data$repetition <- NULL manifest <- create_gazepoint_feature_manifest(   features = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   scientific_source = c(     "Gazepoint fixation export",     "Gazepoint pupil export"   ),   source_table = c("fixations", "pupil"),   transformation = c(     "Trial-level mean",     "Trial-level change"   ),   availability_stage = "during_exposure",   prediction_time_available = TRUE,   preprocessing_scope = "none",   fold_local_required = FALSE ) folds <- create_gazepoint_group_folds(   data = example_data,   outcome = "outcome",   predictors = c("fixation_duration", "pupil_change"),   feature_manifest = manifest,   generalization_target = "new_participants",   participant_id = "participant_id",   trial_id = "trial_id",   stimulus_id = "stimulus_id",   v = 3L,   repeats = 1L,   seed = 101L ) diagnostics <- diagnose_gazepoint_group_folds(folds) print(diagnostics) #> <gazepoint_fold_diagnostics> #> Target: new_participants #> Repeats: 1 #> Folds: 3 #> Outcome type: categorical #> Diagnostic status: PASS #> Maximum assessment-size ratio: 1.000

title: gp3ml 0.1.0
text: Established gp3ml governance-first package leakage-resistant predictive modelling validation using Gazepoint-derived research data. Restricted supported tasks explicitly observed, non-sensitive outcomes clearly declared scientific purposes. Added explicit prohibited-use documentation covering identification, authentication, diagnostic, protected-attribute, emotion, stress, personality, deception, cognition, comprehension, intent, mental-state inference.

title: gp3ml 0.1.0
text: Added task declaration, use-case assertion, variable-role validation, machine-readable prohibited-use helpers. Added feature-provenance manifests covering predictor origins, transformations, availability stages, roles, preprocessing scope. Added structured leakage audits row, participant, participant-trial, stimulus, identifier, target-derived, post-outcome risks.

title: gp3ml 0.1.0
text: Added deterministic group-aware holdout splitting repeated grouped resampling new trials among known participants, new participants, new stimuli, simultaneous new-participant new-stimulus generalization. Materialized analysis, assessment, explicitly excluded partitions complete source-row accounting embedded leakage audits. Added fold-balance, coverage, exclusion, outcome-representation diagnostics structured pass, review, fail findings.

title: gp3ml 0.1.0
text: Added fold-local preprocessing objects separate fitting baking interfaces. Added governed model engines, explicit black-box integration, classification regression metrics, calibration assessment, explicitly labelled bootstrap metric uncertainty. Added external-validation evaluation reporting without treating internal holdout external validation. Added model cards reproducibility reports record task purpose, governance decisions, model settings, performance, calibration, uncertainty, limitations, reproducibility information. Model selection remains explicit reviewable; package perform autonomous black-box winner selection.

title: gp3ml 0.1.0
